bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My top values across all issues are valueD for everything, giving me utility of 0.1*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 1.0. There's no offer history to read, so I'll open strong with my ideal bid.

The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueA), which gives me minimum utility (0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total). We're very early (t=0.02), so I should stay near my top bid. I'll maintain my maximum utility bid since there's no reason to concede yet - the opponent is clearly starting from their ideal position just as I am.

The opponent is consistently offering all valueA (their best, my worst), showing no concession at all. We're very early (t=0.04), so there's no need to concede yet. I'll maintain my top-utility bid (all valueD) since it's still very early in the negotiation and the opponent hasn't moved at all. I'll stay firm to signal that I won't capitulate easily.

The opponent has been offering all-valueA bids (utility ~0.2 for me) but just made a tiny concession on issueD from valueA to valueB. This is very early (t=0.06), so I should stay near my optimal bid. The opponent seems to be holding firm on their preferred values, but the slight movement on issueD (my highest-weight issue) is a positive signal. I'll maintain my strong position but make a small concession on issueA (my lowest-weight issue, 0.1) to signal willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is slowly conceding - they moved issueD from valueA to valueB in one offer, and issueC from valueA to valueB in the latest offer. This suggests they prefer lower values (valueA) and are conceding upward. We're still very early (t=0.08), so I should stay near my top utility while making small concessions. My highest weight issue is issueD (0.4), so I'll hold firm there at valueD. I can slightly concede on issueA (lowest weight, 0.1) by keeping valueC, and make a small concession on issueB to valueC to signal willingness to negotiate, while keeping issueC and issueD at valueD.

The opponent is making very slow concessions - they started at all valueA and have only slightly moved on individual issues (issueD to valueB once, issueC to valueB once, issueB to valueB once). Their utility to me remains very low (~0.2). We're only at t=0.1, so there's no need to rush concessions. I'll hold firm on my most important issues (issueD, issueC) while making a small concession on issueA (low weight) to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll keep issueD and issueC at valueD since they're my highest-weight issues.

The opponent is making very slow concessions - they started at all valueA and have only moved slightly (issueD to valueB, issueC to valueB, issueB to valueB at various points).
